prompt:
Our asset pipeline's include/exclude rules currently go through filepath.Match and users keep filing bugs: no ** support, and ? chokes on accented filenames because somewhere along the line bytes and runes got confused. I want our own matcher we fully control.

Write package glob at the root of module go-glomatch — sorry, go-globmatch. One exported function and one exported error:

  func Match(pattern, name string) (bool, error)
  var ErrBadPattern = errors.New(...)

Semantics:
- literal characters match themselves, case-sensitively; the whole name must be consumed (no implicit anchoring games)
- `*` matches any run (possibly empty) of characters EXCEPT '/'
- `**` matches any run including '/' (so `src/**.go` covers src/main.go and src/a/b/c.go); three stars in a row just means ** followed by *
- `?` matches exactly ONE rune — a rune, not a byte, this is the bug I keep paying for — and never '/'
- character classes: `[abc]`, ranges `[a-c]`, negation with leading `!` like `[!0-9]`; a class consumes exactly one rune
- backslash escapes the next pattern character so you can match literal `*`, `?`, `[`
- malformed patterns (unterminated `[`, empty `[]` or `[!]`, inverted range like `[z-a]`, trailing backslash) return (false, ErrBadPattern) — and I want the error reported even when the name obviously wouldn't have matched anyway, so validate properly instead of bailing at the first mismatch

Backtracking has to actually work (`a*b` against `aXbXb`, multiple stars, etc.).

Ground rules: stdlib only, and do NOT implement this by delegating to path.Match / filepath.Match / regexp — replacing that is the whole point of the ticket. glob_test.go is the acceptance suite; don't touch it. go test -race -timeout 30s ./... green = done.
